claimText: Axiom-4 is a private astronaut mission to the International Space Station conducted by Axiom Space in collaboration with NASA and SpaceX.
evidenceExcerpt: Ax-4 is a 4th private astronaut mission, to the International Space Station (ISS), of private US Company Axiom Space in collaboration with NASA and SpaceX.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nature and collaborators of the Axiom-4 mission.
distractorHooks: Claiming it is a purely government mission by NASA and ISRO
Claiming it is a mission to the Moon or Mars instead of the ISS

claimText: Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla is the first Indian astronaut to board the International Space Station and the second Indian to travel to space.
evidenceExcerpt: Shubhanshu Shukla is the first Indian astronaut to board the ISS and only the second Indian to travel to space after Rakesh Sharma in 1984.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multiple-choice question regarding Indian astronauts' milestones.
distractorHooks: Claiming Rakesh Sharma boarded the ISS
Claiming Shubhanshu Shukla is the first Indian in space

claimText: ISRO executed microgravity research on the Axiom-4 mission involving crop growth, cyanobacteria, space microalgae, myogenesis, and tardigrades.
evidenceExcerpt: Key Research and activities executed by ISRO: Crop growth, Cyanobacteria, Space Microalgae, Myogenesis, Tardigrades.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the specific biological research conducted by ISRO on the Ax-4 mission.
distractorHooks: Attributing the research to NASA or ESA instead of ISRO
Listing incorrect research areas like deep space mining or astrophysics instead of biological research

claimText: The International Space Station (ISS) is a habitable artificial satellite positioned in Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
evidenceExcerpt: About International Space Station (ISS): A habitable artificial satellite in Low Earth orbit (LEO).
examUse: Statement-based question on the orbital characteristics of the ISS.
distractorHooks: Placing the ISS in Geostationary Earth Orbit (GEO)
Placing the ISS in Medium Earth Orbit (MEO)

claimText: The International Space Station is a collaborative project partnered by space agencies from Europe (ESA), the USA (NASA), Japan (JAXA), Canada (CSA), and Russia (Roscosmos).
evidenceExcerpt: Key partners for ISS: Europe (ESA), the USA (NASA), Japan (JAXA), Canada (CSA) and Russia (Roscosmos).
examUse: Multiple-choice question asking to identify the correct participating agencies or countries of the ISS.
distractorHooks: Including ISRO (India) as a key partner of the ISS
Including CNSA (China) as a key partner of the ISS
